response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which made his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David gathered three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. The total number of blueberries they had was 76.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins needed 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make they divided the total blueberries by 9. This gave them 8 batches with 4 blueberries left over. Each batch was sold for $5. So they multiplied the number of batches by the price per batch to find their total earnings. Their total earnings were $40.

***

The leftover blueberries were not used for muffins. They had 4 blueberries remaining. These were not sold and were not used in any other way. The money they made came only from the muffins they sold. The total amount they earned was $40. This money was from the blueberries they collected together.

***

The process of collecting and selling blueberries involved several steps. They first gathered the blueberries then made muffins from them. They sold the muffins and earned money. The money they made was from the muffins they sold. Their total earnings were $40. The youth of the group was evident in their efforts and the money they earned.
